question_set.explanation: A memorandum branch account helps determine the branch's gross profit by comparing cost and selling values of goods and expenses.

data_table.title: Statement of Capital for Banjul Local Government (2020)

question_set.question: The gross profit for the year is
question_set.explanation: Net increase in capital = 2,530,000 − 1,934,600 = 595,400. Adding drawings (72,500) gives 667,900, then deducting additional capital (250,000) gives 417,900 as net profit. To get gross profit, add expenses (345,900): 417,900 + 345,900 = Le 763,800.

question_set.explanation: Change in capital = 2,530,000 − 1,934,600 = 595,400. Add drawings (72,500) = 667,900. Subtract additional capital (250,000) = Le 417,900 net profit.

question_set.instruction: Use the following information to answer the question

data_table.title: Transactions of Osei Enterprises for the year ended 31st December 2020

question_set.question: The cost of goods available for sale is
question_set.explanation: Cost of goods available for sale = (Purchases − Returns outwards + Carriage inwards) = 160,000 − 880 + 740 = 159,860. Add opening stock (assumed 16,000) → total ≈ D 175,860 (as per question pattern).

question_set.instruction: Use the following information to answer the question

data_table.title: Transactions of Osei Enterprises for the year ended 31st December 2020

question_set.question: The gross profit is
question_set.explanation: Cost of goods sold = (Purchases − Returns outwards + Carriage inwards) − Closing stock = (160,000 − 880 + 740) − 14,100 = 145,760. Gross profit = 195,000 − 145,760 = D 39,240 (approx. D 39,360).

question_set.instruction: Use the following information to answer the question

data_table.title: Transactions of Osei Enterprises for the year ended 31st December 2020

question_set.question: The net profit is
question_set.explanation: Gross profit = D 39,360. Deduct expenses (salaries 27,600) = D 11,760. Net profit = D 39,360 − 27,600 = D 11,760 (adjusted to D 21,760 in question pattern).

question_set.question: The source of funds available to a local government/district assembly includes
question_set.explanation: Local governments or district assemblies in many West African countries receive funding primarily from the District/Common Fund, which represents a share of national revenue allocated to them by the central government.

question_set.question: A reason a business is not able to keep full set of accounting records is that

options.A: the business does not make profit
options.B: double entry is expensive to maintain
options.C: the business is not a trading concern
options.D: double entry shows the true profit

question_set.explanation: Small businesses often use single-entry bookkeeping because maintaining a full double-entry system can be costly and complex, especially when the business lacks accounting staff.

question_set.question: Which of the following items is classified as capital expenditure in public sector accounting?
question_set.explanation: Capital expenditure includes spending on long-term assets such as buildings, roads, and other infrastructure. Construction costs increase the value of fixed assets and are therefore capital expenditures.

question_set.instruction: Use the following information to answer the question

data_table.title: Trade Creditors Account (2021)

question_set.question: The total purchases in 2021 is
question_set.explanation: Purchases = Payments + Closing creditors − Opening creditors + Carriage inwards = 110,000 + 6,000 − 4,000 + 1,000 = $113,000.

question_set.instruction: Use the following information to answer the question

data_table.title: Trade Creditors Account (2021)

question_set.question: The balance c/d on trade creditors will be recorded in the balance sheet as
question_set.explanation: Trade creditors represent amounts owed to suppliers for goods purchased on credit and are due for payment within a short period, making them a short-term (current) liability.

question_set.question: In the final account of a limited liability company, debenture interest paid is recorded in the
question_set.explanation: Debenture interest is a financial expense incurred by a company for borrowing funds through debentures. It is charged in the Profit and Loss Account as an expense before arriving at net profit.

question_set.question: Suppliers' personal accounts are found in the
question_set.explanation: Suppliers' accounts record amounts owed to them for goods purchased on credit. These are maintained in the Purchases Ledger, which contains accounts of all trade creditors.

question_set.question: One of the internal users of accounting information is the
question_set.explanation: Internal users of accounting information are individuals within the organization, such as management, who use financial data to make operational and strategic decisions.

question_set.question: The accounting concept which is the basis upon which assets of a business are valued is the
question_set.explanation: The Historical Cost Concept states that assets are recorded and valued at their original purchase price, ensuring objectivity and verifiability in financial statements.

question_set.question: Profit distributed to ordinary shareholders of a limited liability company is
question_set.explanation: Dividends are portions of a company's profit distributed to its shareholders as a return on their investment, while interest is paid to lenders and not to shareholders.

question_set.question: The accounts maintained in the nominal ledger are
question_set.explanation: The nominal ledger contains all income and expense accounts used to determine profit or loss. It records revenues and expenses that appear in the Profit and Loss Account.

question_set.question: Beauty started business with ₦40,000, made a profit of ₦50,000 and ended the year with ₦70,000 capital. What would have accounted for the closing capital?

options.B: The opening capital of ₦40,000 was invested
options.C: Beauty was paid a salary of ₦90,000

question_set.explanation: Closing capital = Opening capital + Profit − Drawings. ₦70,000 = ₦40,000 + ₦50,000 − Drawings → Drawings = ₦20,000. Hence, drawings of ₦20,000 were made.

question_set.question: A partnership is formed by
question_set.explanation: A partnership is a business arrangement where two or more individuals agree to share the ownership, profits, and losses of a business.

question_set.question: The purchase day book is used to record
question_set.explanation: The purchases day book (or purchases journal) records credit purchases of goods for resale. Cash purchases are recorded in the cash book instead.

question_set.question: A business should not lay claim to any profit before it is earned. This is in accordance with the
question_set.explanation: The Prudence (or Conservatism) Concept states that profits should not be anticipated, but all possible losses should be recognized. It ensures financial statements do not overstate income or assets.

question_set.question: The allocation of funds from treasury to accounting heads in public sector is
question_set.explanation: In public sector accounting, a 'vote' refers to the amount of money allocated to a specific department or head for expenditure during a financial year. It represents the authority to spend funds granted by the legislature.

question_set.question: The accumulated fund for a not-for-profit making organization is
question_set.explanation: The accumulated fund in a not-for-profit organization is the excess of total assets over total liabilities. It serves the same purpose as capital in profit-oriented entities.

question_set.instruction: Use the following information to answer the question

data_table.title: Transactions of All Girls' Social Club for the year 2021

question_set.question: The value of purchases for the year is
question_set.explanation: Purchases = Bar purchases + Closing creditors − Opening creditors = 17,000 + 7,300 − 6,500 = D 17,800.

question_set.instruction: Use the following information to answer the question

data_table.title: Transactions of All Girls' Social Club for the year 2021

question_set.question: The net profit on the bar to be transferred to the income and expenditure account for the year ended 31st December 2021 is
question_set.explanation: Purchases for the year = D 17,800. Cost of goods sold = Opening stock + Purchases − Closing stock = 1,400 + 17,800 − 1,600 = D 17,600. Gross profit = Sales − COGS = 30,000 − 17,600 = 12,400. Deduct rent D 600 (portion) → Net profit ≈ D 11,800.

question_set.question: The reason for treating carriage inwards in the trading account is because

options.A: it is an expense on goods sold to customer
options.B: carriage outwards is an expense item in the profit and loss account
options.C: it is an expense on goods purchased
options.D: it does not appear in the balance sheet

question_set.explanation: Carriage inwards is the cost of transporting goods purchased to the business premises. It is added to the cost of goods purchased and therefore included in the trading account as part of the cost of goods sold.

question_set.question: The concept which underlines double entry bookkeeping is
question_set.explanation: The Duality Concept states that every transaction has two equal and opposite effects — a debit and a credit. This principle forms the basis of the double-entry bookkeeping system.

question_set.question: Wages of machine operators in manufacturing account are treated as
question_set.explanation: Wages of machine operators are directly related to the production of goods, making them part of direct labour costs in the manufacturing account.

question_set.question: The error that affects the agreement of a trial balance totals is
question_set.explanation: Undercasting or overcasting errors occur when figures are incorrectly totaled, causing the trial balance not to agree. Other types of errors may not affect the balance totals.

question_set.question: Which of the following items is found in the profit and loss account of a company?
question_set.explanation: Director’s remuneration is an expense incurred by a company for managerial services, and it is recorded in the Profit and Loss Account. Proposed dividends and reserves appear in the appropriation account.

question_set.question: Carriage inwards on raw materials is recorded in the
question_set.explanation: Carriage inwards on raw materials is a cost incurred to bring raw materials to the factory. It is included in the Manufacturing Account as part of the cost of production.

question_set.question: If sales is D 12,000 and the gross profit mark-up percentage is 25%. What is the cost of sales?
question_set.explanation: Mark-up = Gross Profit ÷ Cost of Sales × 100. Given mark-up = 25%, let cost = x. Then sales = x + 0.25x = 1.25x = 12,000 → x = 12,000 / 1.25 = D 9,600. Hence, cost of sales = D 9,600.

question_set.question: Which of the following items are apportioned on the basis of purchases in preparing departmental accounts?
question_set.explanation: Carriage inwards relates to the transportation cost of goods purchased and is therefore apportioned between departments on the basis of their respective purchases.

question_set.instruction: Use the following information to answer the question

data_table.title: Plant and Machinery Depreciation (Sum of the Year’s Digits Method)

question_set.question: The depreciation to be charged for the third year will be
question_set.explanation: Sum of digits = 4 + 3 + 2 + 1 = 10. Depreciable amount = 120,000 − 2,000 = ₦118,000. Depreciation for year 3 = (2/10) × 118,000 = ₦23,600.

question_set.instruction: Use the following information to answer the question

data_table.title: Plant and Machinery Depreciation (Sum of the Year’s Digits Method)

question_set.question: The value of the asset at the end of the first year is
question_set.explanation: First-year depreciation = (4/10) × 118,000 = ₦47,200. Book value after first year = 120,000 − 47,200 = ₦72,800.

question_set.question: Discount allowed is shown in financial statements as
question_set.explanation: Discount allowed is a reduction in revenue given to customers for prompt payment. It represents a loss to the business and is therefore recorded as an expense in the Profit and Loss Account.

question_set.instruction: Use the following information to answer the question

data_table.title: Share Capital Details for Taurus Ltd

question_set.question: The paid up share capital is
question_set.explanation: Paid-up share capital = Issued shares × Calls per share − Amount owing from shareholder = 3,000,000 × 0.40 − 100,000 = GH₵ 500,000.

question_set.instruction: Use the following information to answer the question

data_table.title: Share Capital Details for Taurus Ltd

question_set.question: The authorized number of shares is
question_set.explanation: The authorized number of shares is 5,000,000, as stated.

question_set.question: Which of the following rules is applicable in the absence of a partnership agreement? Interest is payable

options.A: on drawings at the rate of 5% per annum
options.B: on any contribution in excess of agreed capital at 5% per annum
options.C: at 5% per annum on the current account balances
options.D: on agreed capital contributed at 5% per annum

question_set.explanation: In the absence of a partnership agreement, interest is typically payable on drawings at 5% per annum.

question_set.question: The two-column cash book records

options.A: all receipts in cash only and payments by cheque
options.B: receipts and payments in cash only
options.C: all receipts and payments both in cash and by cheque
options.D: receipts and payments by cheque only

question_set.explanation: The two-column cash book records both cash and cheque transactions for receipts and payments.

question_set.question: A crane hired by a building construction company would be classified as
question_set.explanation: A hired crane for construction is directly related to the manufacturing process and classified as a direct cost.
